title: Telis cachemiriana
intro: Telis cachemiriana, also known as Himalayan telis, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to the Himalayan region. The health benefits of Telis cachemiriana include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Telis cachemiriana include decoctions, infusions, and powder extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Telis cachemiriana is classified within the family Cynipidae, which is a group of gall wasps. The genus Telis is part of the subfamily Charipinae. More specifically, Telis cachemiriana belongs to the species level, and its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Animalia, Phylum: Arthropoda, Class: Insecta, Order: Hymenoptera, Family: Cynipidae, Subfamily: Charipinae, Genus: Telis, Species: T. cachemiriana. This species is a type of gall wasp that induces galls on plants. The exact classification of Telis cachemiriana may vary depending on the source, but the above classification is generally accepted.
